method: Scenarios designed for W12 curveball drill; scored on the REAL engine (STL + EWMA, alpha=0.3, K=4.0), sliding 120-min window step 5. Deterministic (seed=20260701), reproducible.

curveball_1_small.scenario: New service 'checkout-svc' onboarded mid-incident with NO trained baseline; a real gradual CPU exhaustion follows.
curveball_1_small.twist: No STL baseline -> engine must fall back to in-window z-score (graceful degradation).

curveball_2_medium.scenario: Planned Flash Sale: payment-gw CPU jumps to a sustained higher plateau (+12pp) that never breaches the 90% SLO (peak 50.3%).
curveball_2_medium.twist: Structural, benign shift vs baseline -> known false-positive risk (ADR-003).
curveball_2_medium.lesson: Engine correctly flags the sustained deviation but has no business-calendar context; mitigate with manual 'Silence & Retrain' during known events (ADR-003).

curveball_3_chaos.scenario: Correlated cascade: payment-gw memory leak triggers a downstream fraud-detector CPU ramp, all under 2x-3x telemetry noise, plus a ledger pure-noise distractor.
curveball_3_chaos.twist: Multi-service, multi-metric, noisy; must catch the 2 real drifts AND not false-alarm on the noise-only distractor.
